intro: Polymeria mollis, also known as Mulla Mulla, is a plant species native to Australia that belongs to the family Compositae (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The bioactive constituents of Polymeria mollis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Polymeria mollis include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
